    SQLDataType.BIGINT.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");
Explanation: The table uses a generic 'ID' column as primary key when a natural key already exists via the unique constraint on USERNAME. Consider using USERNAME as the primary key or renaming the ID column to something more descriptive like 'APP_USER_ID'.

Using selectFrom(APP_USER) fetches all columns from the APP_USER table into an AppUserRecord, which may retrieve more data than needed. Consider using select() with only the specific columns required (e.g., USERNAME, PASSWORD_HASH, ENABLED, SECRET) to improve query performance.
